title: Military Technology
description: Cost growth in the development and fielding of technologically advanced weapon systems has become a major economic burden for many nations and is expected to be an enduring and prevalent problem. RAND research has provided cost analyses and recommendations to help policymakers and military leaders develop improved cost-estimating tools and formulate policies that mitigate cost growth in military technology acquisition practices.
text:
Why Korea's Nuclear-Powered Submarine Matters to U.S. Strategy
A nuclear-powered submarine would not just be a Korean capability upgrade. In an era of intensifying undersea competition and growing demands on U.S. forces, this is a strategic opportunity for the alliance, as well as for security in the Indo-Pacific.
Surveying Duality in Space: Volume III, Indo-Pacific Workshop Insights on Developing, Deploying, and Governing Dual-Use Space Systems
These proceedings share details on a workshop examining dual-use space systems (those capable of performing both civilian and military operations) that included space stakeholders from the Indo-Pacific from government, private, and research sectors.
Duality in Space
RAND researchers discuss the challenges of defining and governing dual-use space systems—technologies that can serve both civilian and military purposes—across geographic regions and political contexts.
Surveying Duality in Space: Volume II, Europe-Eurasia Workshop Insights on Developing, Deploying, and Governing Dual-Use Space Systems
These proceedings share details on a workshop about dual-use space systems (those capable of performing both civilian and military operations) with space stakeholders from Europe and the Middle East from government, private, and research sectors.
Surveying Duality in Space: Volume I, Americas Workshop Insights on Developing, Deploying, and Governing Dual-Use Space Systems
These proceedings provide details on a workshop examining dual-use space systems (those capable of performing both civilian and military operations) that included space stakeholders from the Americas from government, private, and research sectors.
How Artificial Intelligence Could Reshape Four Essential Competitions in Future Warfare
The authors offer a conceptual framework and preliminary assessment of how artificial intelligence could affect four “building block” competitions in military affairs.
Enabling NATO Digital Capabilities: Lessons from Finnish investment in digital capabilities for defence
This case study, one of four, examines Finland’s approach to digital capabilities. It identifies lessons that can be drawn from Finland’s experience and approach.
Poland's Digital Defence Landscape: Lessons from Polish investment in digital capabilities for defence
This case study, one of four, examines Poland’s approach to digital capabilities. It identifies lessons that can be drawn from Poland’s experience and approach.
Small State, Digital Giant: Lessons from Estonian investment in digital capabilities for defence
This case study, one of four, examines Estonia’s approach to digital capabilities. It identifies lessons that can be drawn from Estonia’s experience and approach.
Enabling NATO Digital Capabilities: Lessons from Swedish investment in digital capabilities for defence
This case study, one of four, examines Sweden’s approach to digital capabilities. It identifies lessons that can be drawn from Sweden’s experience and approach.
Evaluating Digital Modernization: Enhancing Analytical and Decision Support in Department of Defense Acquisition Management
The U.S. Department of Defense (DoD) is pursuing digital acquisition to leverage digital technologies and methodologies for enhanced acquisition outcomes. This report fulfills a congressional mandate to assess the state of DoD digital acquisition.
Order in Orbit: Operationalizing an International Space Traffic Management Organization
The need for effective international space traffic management has never been more pressing. In this report, the authors provide a roadmap for moving from the existing decentralized context to a common international space traffic management system.
